findings.blocker: Polymorphic-scalar pattern unification fails through the anonymous-closure codomain of `Pi.lift`. Six iter-099 routes were exhausted; six iter-100 routes (S1 `set h_sgn : k`, S2 `Linear.smul_comp`, ℤ-scalar `set h_sgn : ℤ` + `rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`, `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`, `Preadditive.nsmul_comp`, `change`-with-ascription, `set f' :=`) all FAIL at the same level. `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` works in standalone `lean_run_code` tests on concrete shapes but the discrimination tree cannot locate the smul-occurrence in the goal in situ. Iter-100 pivoted to `funext j'` (per-coordinate strategy); structural step landed but residual per-coordinate R-linearity deferred.

Diagnostic: `set h_sgn : ℤ := …` DOES substitute, confirming the scalar elaborates through the ℤ-SMul instance on `(∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ ∏ᶜ (anonymous-closure)`, not the k-scalar instance.

attempts.insight: Plan directive's recommendation `: k` was off-target: the scalar is elaborated in ℤ via the Preadditive ZSMul instance, not in k. Iter-100 plan should have used ℤ. This is a meta-lesson — `lean_multi_attempt` against the actual goal IS needed before transcribing a recipe verbatim.

Same for `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`, `simp_rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`, `dsimp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`, and an explicit `rw [h_test]` with locally bound `h_test : ∀ M N (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N), (n • f).hom = n • f.hom := rfl`.

This is a deep discrimination-tree issue, not a missing-lemma issue.

attempts.goal_after: case h.h (per-coordinate): e₂ (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))) j' = (r' • e₂ (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e₁.symm y')))) j'
attempts.insight: PARTIAL STRUCTURAL ADVANCE — the per-coordinate equation isolates a single Z₂ j' output, where the eventual R-action is concretely `(presheaf.map _).hom` via `RingHom.toModule`, allowing `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` to apply directly without needing to push the `(-1)^↑i •` through the entire chain. Iter-101+ closes via: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` → `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j'` → `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` (categorical fuse) → eqToHom-naturality on `eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j'` → `Pi.lift_π_apply` → `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. The `(-1)^↑i •` scalar commutes symmetrically and factors via `congr 1` / `mul_smul` at the end.

next_steps: Iter-101 plan-agent action: schedule prover on `BasicOpenCech.lean` to close the per-coordinate residual post-funext at L768. Detailed 6-step recipe in `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md` § 'Concrete next steps for iter-101+': (1) `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` on RHS; (2) `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j'`; (3) `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to fuse `Pi.π Z₂ j' ∘ eqToHom`; (4) eqToHom-naturality + `Pi.lift_π_apply`; (5) `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` for the per-coordinate R-linearity; (6) `congr 1` / `mul_smul` to factor out the (-1)^↑i scalar. Alternative escalation: refactor `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` to use `Pi.lift_thing` as a let-binder at the body level, so the family `G : ι' → (∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z_int)` is matched against the let-binder rather than the literal anonymous-closure — this sidesteps the discrimination-tree issue at the call site. The prover's three suggested iter-101 escalation paths are recorded in the task result.
